name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. provides cloud-based digital workflow solutions to enterprises globally. Its Now Platform connects IT, employee, and customer workflows into a single AI-powered system of action. ServiceNow serves as the operating system for enterprise work — connecting people, data, and automation across departments. The company manages IT service management, HR service delivery, customer service management, security operations, and financial services workflows. Q1 FY2026 subscription revenues were $3.671B (up 22% YoY), with Now Assist (AI) reaching $750M in annual contract value and a $1.5B ACV target for 2026. ServiceNow holds a $105B+ market cap and management has articulated a path to $30B+ in annual revenue. The company has deep partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic, using Claude models across customer workflows and internal productivity tools.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has executed a transformative pivot from ITSM ticketing tool to 'the AI control tower for enterprise business reinvention' — a multi-year platform expansion that redefined the company's addressable market from IT departments to every workflow in the enterprise [2]. Now Assist went from concept to $600M+ ACV in 2025, more than doubling year-over-year [2]. The company has consistently expanded from IT into HR, customer service, security, and financial workflows, growing the platform TAM with each adjacent entry. This is multi-dimensional execution, not incremental product refresh. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist AI is generating $750M in annual contract value as of Q1 2026, with management raising the full-year target from $1B to $1.5B ACV [2]. ServiceNow has deepened integration with Anthropic Claude models for mission-critical customer workflows in healthcare and financial services, and has a strategic multiyear partnership with OpenAI [3]. AI is generating measurable, quantified revenue — not just cost savings. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the category leader in enterprise workflow automation with no credible head-to-head competitor at its scale; the platform depth across ITSM, HRSD, CSM, and security operations creates switching costs that make share loss structurally difficult [4]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Management has articulated a clear path to $30B+ in subscription revenue and stated that 30% of ACV could be pure AI revenue by 2030 (~$9B AI ACV at a 56% CAGR from 2026-2030) [2]. The pipeline is directionally compelling but specific next-generation product names and launch dates beyond Now Assist are not as precisely articulated. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D expense data not available in yfinance; ServiceNow's operating margins of 13.3% and EBITDA margins of 20.7% suggest meaningful reinvestment, but specific R&D-to-revenue ratio is not confirmed above industry average.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — to make the world of work work better for people — is specific and thoughtful, connecting enterprise software to human dignity in the workplace [1]. Under Bill McDermott's leadership, the company has elevated its vision to 'become the defining enterprise AI software company of the 21st century,' which is bold and directional. The mission earns 3 points: it is clearly thought through, distinctive from generic enterprise software rhetoric, and connects to a larger purpose. It falls short of 4 because the multi-decade validation test is incomplete — McDermott joined in 2019. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow has established philanthropic programs — the ServiceNow.org initiative and scholarship programs — but specific dollar amounts and distinctive community investment programs are not prominently featured at the top-tier level [1]. Adequate, not exceptional. Employee Retention (1/2): ServiceNow's 29,187 employees operate in an environment that industry observers describe as mission-driven, with relatively low voluntary turnover for an enterprise software company. McDermott has maintained senior leadership stability since his 2019 arrival [1]. However, specific tenure or turnover data is not publicly disclosed. Insider Buying (1/2): Insider ownership is only 0.17%, and no specific net insider buying was identified in the last 12 months [5]. However, the proxy statement reflects that McDermott's compensation is heavily equity-based with performance conditions tied to multi-year revenue and ACV targets, aligning incentives with long-term platform success [1].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: ServiceNow's financial infrastructure is exceptional by growth stock standards. Mechanically scored: D/E of 20.7% scores 1.0 (well below 70% target, reflecting $5.18B cash against $2.43B debt) [5]. ROE of 16.1% scores 1.0 (above 15% target) [5]. Revenue Growth YoY of 22.1% scores 1.0 [2]. 3-year Revenue CAGR of 22.4% (2022: $7.245B to 2025: $13.278B) scores 1.0 [5]. Forward P/E of 20.3x scores 0.99 (essentially at target of 20x) [5]. Profit Margin of 12.6% scores 0.63 (between 0% floor and 20% target) — improvement expected as AI mix increases higher-margin ACV [5]. Forward PEG of 0.97 scores 1.0 (below 1.0 target — growth is underpriced relative to PEG) [5]. Weighted infrastructure score: 9.62/10 → rounds to 10. Liquidity is ample: avg daily volume 24M shares × $102 price = $2.46B daily liquidity.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): ServiceNow is not threatened by AI — it is the platform through which AI gets deployed in enterprises. The Now Platform serves as the 'AI control tower' that orchestrates AI agents, workflow automation, and human oversight across enterprise operations [2]. AI disruption would require enterprises to rip out and replace deeply embedded, mission-critical workflow systems that have years of customization and process data — a structurally implausible substitution. Analysts who worried about AI risk for ServiceNow have been proven wrong: instead of disrupting NOW workflows, AI is deepening enterprise reliance on the platform by making it more valuable [4]. ServiceNow's partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic make the company an AI distribution channel, not an AI target. The company is deliberately building itself into the control layer for agentic AI in enterprises. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist generated $750M in annual contract value in Q1 2026, with management raising the full-year target to $1.5B ACV [2]. This is not potential AI revenue — it is contracted, recognized AI revenue growing at double-digit rates. ServiceNow management has stated a 30% AI ACV target by 2030, implying ~$9B in pure AI annual revenue. This is among the most concrete AI revenue stories in enterprise software.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow scores well as an infinite game player — the platform strategy, long-term AI vision, and consistent investment in platform depth over short-term earnings reflect genuinely long-horizon thinking. McDermott's willingness to make bold long-term revenue statements ($30B+ target, 30% AI ACV by 2030) while investing through a period of AI anxiety reflects infinite game conviction. The company frames its competitive position not as 'winning against SAP or Salesforce' but as 'defining the AI enterprise software category' — a subtle but meaningful distinction.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's purpose — to make the world of work work better for people — and the elevated vision to become 'the defining enterprise AI software company of the 21st century' represent a genuine and distinctive cause [1]. McDermott consistently connects ServiceNow's platform work to human productivity and organizational dignity. The cause is specific enough to shape strategic decisions: ServiceNow has invested in adjacencies (HR, customer service, security) that expand human workflow value rather than purely optimizing IT cost. However, the company is only 7 years into McDermott's tenure and has not yet had the decades-long test that characterizes Sinek's top-tier Just Cause examples.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): ServiceNow has demonstrated a consistent pattern of courageous long-term bets. When AI anxiety caused Wall Street to sell the stock on concerns that AI would disrupt ITSM workflows, management doubled down on AI investment and launched Now Assist rather than defensively cutting spend [4]. The decision to raise the AI ACV target from $1B to $1.5B mid-year — when macroeconomic uncertainty was elevated — reflects conviction over caution. The multi-year OpenAI and Anthropic partnerships required significant capital commitment before return was certain. Management's public statement of a path to $30B+ in revenue at a time when the stock was under pressure is a genuinely courageous act [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow's 29,187-person organization has maintained strong cultural cohesion under McDermott's tenure since 2019. The company is consistently cited in enterprise software communities as a high-engagement employer with strong mission alignment. Leadership stability at the senior level is notable — most of the executive team has tenure exceeding five years under McDermott. The company's investment in employee development programs and ServiceNow.org social impact initiatives signal values-driven management. No evidence of high-turnover culture, fear-based management, or significant organizational disruption [1].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow has demonstrated meaningful business model evolution — expanding from a pure ITSM ticketing vendor to a full enterprise workflow platform, and now pivoting to position itself as the AI control tower for enterprise operations [2]. The addition of Now Assist and the OpenAI/Anthropic partnerships represent genuine strategic evolution rather than incremental feature additions. However, ServiceNow has not yet made the kind of painful, profitable-business-abandonment pivot that earns the top score. The core platform model remains intact; AI is additive, not transformative of the business structure. This may be appropriate — not all companies need to blow up their business — but it caps the existential flexibility score.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): ServiceNow's competitive language consistently focuses on building the best platform for enterprise AI rather than defeating SAP, Salesforce, or Microsoft. McDermott has framed the competitive dynamic as 'Silicon Valley is getting enterprise AI wrong' — using competitors' approaches to clarify ServiceNow's differentiated vision of AI embedded in mission-critical workflows [2]. This is textbook worthy rival thinking: using the competitive landscape to sharpen your own identity rather than obsessing over beating specific players.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the most compelling AI-native enterprise platform in the market — not because it builds AI models, but because it provides the workflow orchestration layer through which AI agents are deployed, governed, and measured inside enterprises [2]. Now Assist reached $750M in ACV in Q1 2026, more than doubled year-over-year, with management raising the full-year target to $1.5B [2]. Q1 2026 subscription revenues of $3.671B grew 22% YoY, consistent with multi-year growth trends. The Forward PEG of 0.97 — below 1.0 — is unusual for a company growing at 22% with 22%+ revenue CAGR over three years, suggesting the market is underpricing the AI monetization trajectory. Management has outlined a credible path to $30B+ in revenue and stated that 30% of ACV could be pure AI revenue by 2030 (~$9B AI ACV) [2].
thesis_continued: The primary risks are valuation sensitivity and competitive moat erosion at the edges. The trailing P/E of 60.8x and market cap of $105B+ price in sustained excellence — any execution miss on the $1.5B AI ACV target or deterioration in the 22% subscription growth rate could cause meaningful multiple compression [5]. Profit margins of 12.6% are below the 20% target despite strong gross margins (76.6%), reflecting significant ongoing investment in sales, marketing, and R&D — margin expansion is a key 2026-2027 earnings driver that carries execution risk. Microsoft's Copilot for ServiceNow workflows and Salesforce's Agentforce platform represent long-term competitive threats at the edges of ServiceNow's enterprise footprint, though neither has penetrated the core ITSM and workflow automation category at scale [4]. The Inspire score of -49 (MISALIGNED) reflects LGBT activism attributions that may be material for faith-aligned investors.
key_catalysts: Now Assist ACV growth toward the $1.5B 2026 target — each $250M of incremental AI ACV at ServiceNow's gross margins adds ~$190M in gross profit, directly driving operating leverage and margin expansion

Agentic AI platform adoption at enterprise scale — ServiceNow's positioning as the AI control tower for enterprise operations becomes more valuable as AI agents proliferate, deepening the switching cost moat and expanding wallet share

Operating margin expansion from 13.3% toward 20%+ as Now Assist ACV grows and fixed infrastructure costs are absorbed, potentially driving significant EPS growth that justifies or grows into the current 60x trailing P/E [5]
key_risks: Failure to achieve $1.5B Now Assist ACV by year-end 2026 would signal AI monetization headwinds and could cause 20-30% multiple compression from current levels given the premium embedded in the valuation

Microsoft Copilot and Salesforce Agentforce expanding into enterprise workflow orchestration could commoditize elements of ServiceNow's platform at the edges, pressuring net new ACV growth in HR service delivery and customer service modules

Profit margin of 12.6% remains well below the 20% target; if sales and marketing intensity needs to increase to defend market position against AI-native competitors, the path to margin expansion could lengthen, compressing the earnings growth narrative [5]
sources: ServiceNow Form DEF 14A (Proxy Statement) FY2026 — Mission, McDermott Leadership, Compensation Structure — SEC, April 2026
ServiceNow Q1 FY2026 Earnings (Form 8-K) — Now Assist $750M ACV, 22% Revenue Growth, $1.5B Target — SEC, April 2026; Fortune — ServiceNow $30B Revenue Path, May 6, 2026
Fast Company — ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott: Silicon Valley Is Getting Enterprise AI Wrong, 2026; AIBusiness — OpenAI ServiceNow Strategic Partnership
Yahoo Finance / Insider Monkey — ServiceNow AI Fears Selloff Analysis, Competitive Edge Coverage, 2026
